description:
You are acting as a cloud-solutions architect who is preparing a
 migration plan for a set of micro-services that will be moved to a
 brand-new Kubernetes cluster.

 The directory /home/user/migration already contains three data files
 that were exported from the company’s CMDB:

 1. /home/user/migration/prod_services.csv
    • Comma-separated.
    • Header row is:  
      service_id,service_name,current_env,replicas,owner

 2. /home/user/migration/staging_services.csv  
    • Same structure and header as the production file.

 3. /home/user/migration/new_cluster_ips.txt  
    • Plain text, one IPv4 address per line, no header.

 TASK  
 Using nothing more than classic Unix text-processing utilities
 (specifically the cut(1) and paste(1) family of commands – you may
 chain them with sort, wc, head, tail, etc. as helpers, but you must
 rely on cut to extract columns and paste to merge them):

 1. Read both *.csv files, extract only the first and second columns
    (service_id and service_name).  
    • The production file must be processed first, followed immediately
      by the staging file; do NOT sort the rows – keep the original
      order found in each file.
 2. Consume IP addresses from new_cluster_ips.txt sequentially, starting
    with the first line of that file, pairing them 1-for-1 with the
    extracted services.
 3. Add a final column called environment that must contain the literal
    string “production” for rows that originated in prod_services.csv
    and “staging” for rows that originated in staging_services.csv.
 4. Assemble everything into a **tab-delimited** output file named
    /home/user/migration/deployment_plan.tsv whose exact first five
    lines must be:

        service_id<TAB>service_name<TAB>target_ip<TAB>environment
        svc-101<TAB>auth-service<TAB>10.240.0.10<TAB>production
        svc-102<TAB>billing-service<TAB>10.240.0.11<TAB>production
        svc-103<TAB>search-service<TAB>10.240.0.12<TAB>production
        svc-201<TAB>auth-service<TAB>10.240.0.13<TAB>staging

    (Where <TAB> represents a real horizontal tab character; **do not
    insert spaces**).  
    The file must continue with any remaining rows produced by the same
    algorithm, following the exact tab layout.

 5. Create a plain-text log named /home/user/migration/plan_generation.log
    with the following verification keys, **in exactly this order**,
    one per line, no extra spaces, no quotes:

        Total_prod_services=<NUMBER>
        Total_staging_services=<NUMBER>
        Total_services=<NUMBER>
        IPs_available=<NUMBER>
        IPs_assigned=<NUMBER>

    The values must be computed dynamically from the input files:
     • Total_prod_services  — data rows (excluding header) in prod file  
     • Total_staging_services — data rows in staging file  
     • Total_services       — sum of the above two counts  
     • IPs_available        — total lines in new_cluster_ips.txt  
     • IPs_assigned         — must equal Total_services

 6. The work must be completed using **5–10 shell commands** total
    (counting pipes and redirections as part of a command), all executed
    inside the /home/user/migration directory.

 When finished, both deployment_plan.tsv and plan_generation.log must
 exist in /home/user/migration with the exact formats described above
 so that an automated test can verify them byte-for-byte.
truth:
Existing input files and their full contents before the task starts:

 1. /home/user/migration/prod_services.csv
    service_id,service_name,current_env,replicas,owner
    svc-101,auth-service,production,3,team-login
    svc-102,billing-service,production,2,team-payment
    svc-103,search-service,production,4,team-search

 2. /home/user/migration/staging_services.csv
    service_id,service_name,current_env,replicas,owner
    svc-201,auth-service,staging,1,team-login
    svc-202,billing-service,staging,1,team-payment

 3. /home/user/migration/new_cluster_ips.txt
    10.240.0.10
    10.240.0.11
    10.240.0.12
    10.240.0.13
    10.240.0.14

 Expected output file #1:
/home/user/migration/deployment_plan.tsv
    (note: each <TAB> below is an actual tab character)

    service_id<TAB>service_name<TAB>target_ip<TAB>environment
    svc-101<TAB>auth-service<TAB>10.240.0.10<TAB>production
    svc-102<TAB>billing-service<TAB>10.240.0.11<TAB>production
    svc-103<TAB>search-service<TAB>10.240.0.12<TAB>production
    svc-201<TAB>auth-service<TAB>10.240.0.13<TAB>staging
    svc-202<TAB>billing-service<TAB>10.240.0.14<TAB>staging

 Expected output file #2:
/home/user/migration/plan_generation.log
    Total_prod_services=3
    Total_staging_services=2
    Total_services=5
    IPs_available=5
    IPs_assigned=5
